Note.title: Note
Note.description: Represents a single note within the Evergreen Notes application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Note entity.

body.description: The main content or body of the note.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the note was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the note was last updated.

UserProgress.title: UserProgress
UserProgress.description: Tracks the user's progress and streak within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProgress entity.

decksCompleted.description: The number of decks the user has completed.

topicsCompleted.description: The number of topics the user has completed.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the Evergreen Notes application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The email address of the user.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  Allows users to only read/write their own document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores notes specific to each user. Path-based authorization ensures only the owner can access these notes.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique ID of the note.

definition.description: Stores user progress data.  Document ID is equal to the userId. This enforces a 1:1 relationship between users and their progress.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.  This is also the document ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). It also leverages denormalization to avoid complex security rules that rely on `get()` calls.

**Structure Overview:**

*   **`/users/{userId}`:** Stores user profiles. Security rules ensure that only the user can read/write their document.
*   **`/users/{userId}/notes/{noteId}`:**  Stores notes specific to each user. This hierarchical structure enforces ownership and simplifies security rules.
*   **`/user_progress/{userId}`:** Stores the user progress. The document ID will be the `userId` to ensure a 1:1 relationship.

**Authorization Independence:**

Authorization is primarily path-based, particularly for notes.  The `/users/{userId}/notes/{noteId}` path inherently grants the specified user ownership. Because there are no shared notes and all notes are private, no denormalization is required.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The structure inherently supports secure `list` operations. Listing notes under `/users/{userId}/notes` is secure because security rules can easily restrict access to only the authenticated `userId`.

**DBAC (Database-Based Access Control):**

Roles are not directly implemented in this design, as the application focuses on private notes per user. If collaborative features were added, a `members` map within each note document (denormalized with user roles) would be necessary to achieve Authorization Independence.
